note: Seed 0 shows a clear '20 CENT'. The upper coin (no green circle) shows a clear '10' = 10cent. Strong bluish cast over gravel background; both numerals readable on zoom. Hough-verified extra at (410,413) r=94; size ratio 20c/10c consistent.

Blue carpet background with heavy cast. Seed 0: big '2' + map, bimetallic, outer ring silver = 2euro. Seed 1: clear '1' + map, bimetallic = 1euro (gold ring, tone washed by cast). Seed 2 sits on a bare dark stone patch top-right, no coin = FP. Size ratio seed0/seed1 = 1.09, matches 2euro/1euro.

Seed 1 shows a clear '10'. Seed 0 is a dark copper-face coin (globe+rays common side), numeral unreadable; radius 76 vs 10cent's 71 gives ~21.2mm = 5cent by relative size. Right coin has NO seed: visible bimetallic inner ring, head profile on national side, r=83 -> ~23.1mm = 1euro (silver ring); could conceivably be 50cent but the concentric ring favors 1euro.

Seed 0: clear '20'. Seed 1: clear '50' + CENT. Seed 2: clear '1' + 'EURO' text, bimetallic = 1euro; odd tones under cast (center looks pale cream, gold outer ring appears dark greenish) but the numeral is unmistakable. Relative radii 98/103/104 consistent with 20c < 50c ~ 1euro.

Seed 0: clear '5' + 'EURO CENT' + globe = 5cent. Seed 1: clear '50'. Lower-middle dark coin has NO seed: bimetallic rim visible, design nearly unreadable (faint diagonal stroke, national side); radius 91 vs 50cent's 86 gives ~25.7mm = 2euro (silver ring, very dark/tarnished). 1euro would be smaller than the 50cent, this coin is larger.
Single copper-face coin (globe with rays, 'EURO CENT' text), rotated ~90deg; big numeral '5' readable after contrast enhancement = 5cent. Hough fit (389,559) r=85 matches the green seed circle.
